options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: BasicResearchLocationCount
options.description: The number of basic research locations in the game. These will only require the basic research bench.

options.name: HiTechResearchLocationCount
options.description: The number of hi-tech research locations in the game. These will only require the hi-tech research bench (which requires Microelectronics.)

options.name: MultiAnalyzerResearchLocationCount
options.description: The number of multi-analyzer research locations in the game. These will only require the hi-tech research bench and the multi-analyzer (and the research for them.)

options.name: ResearchBaseCost
options.description: The amount of research points required to research research locations.

options.name: ResearchMaxPrerequisites
options.description: The max number of prerequisites for the generated Archipelago research. The higher this is, the more restricted your selection of research will be.

options.name: CraftLocationCount
options.description: The number of craft locations in the game. These locations require crafting two random items together.

options.name: RaidLocationCount
options.description: The number of raid locations in the game. These locations require defeating a target enemy, one of which will carry a location item in its inventory in each raid.

options.name: TradeLocationCount
options.description: The number of trade locations in the game. These locations require trading with a caravan or settlement for a location item.

options.name: RaidTrapCount
options.description: The guaranteed number of raid traps in the game.

options.name: ColonistItemCount
options.description: The number of colonists that will get sent as items in the game. Note: If you wind up with more than ~20 colonists, you may have performance issues.

options.name: PlayerNamesAsColonistItems
options.display_name: Use Player Names for Colonist Items
options.description: If enabled, random player names from the multiworld will be used for colonist nicknames

options.name: PercentFillerAsTraps
options.description: The chance random filler will become trap items, like raids.

options.name: VictoryCondition
options.description: The way to win. Choosing "any" will ensure one of these is considered "in-logic." They also require a "basic" set of research that isn't strictly required to win, so royalty will consider pianos and noble apparel in logic, while anomaly will consider basic bioferrite research in logic. The monument victory condition will require building a room with special archipelago statues and some number of randomized room conditions.

options.name: MonumentStatueCount
options.description: If the monument victory condition is enabled, this specifies how many Archipelago statues will be required to create a monument and win the game.

options.name: ExtraMonumentStatueCount
options.description: If the monument victory condition is enabled, this specifies how many extra monument statues will be in the pool, allowing the player an easier victory.

options.name: MonumentOtherBuildingRequirementCount
options.description: If the monument victory condition is enabled, this specifies how many non-statue building requirements will be required to create a monument and win the game.

options.name: MonumentWealthRequirement
options.description: If the monument victory condition is enabled, this specifies how much wealth is required in the monument room to win.

options.name: RoyaltyEnabled
options.description: Enable the Royalty DLC. If you disable any DLC in yaml and enable it in client, all research will be researchable and excluded from the generator.

options.name: IdeologyEnabled
options.description: Enable the Ideology DLC. If you disable any DLC in yaml and enable it in client, all research will be researchable and excluded from the generator.

options.name: BiotechEnabled
options.description: Enable the Biotech DLC. If you disable any DLC in yaml and enable it in client, all research will be researchable and excluded from the generator.

options.name: AnomalyEnabled
options.description: Enable the Anomaly DLC. If you disable any DLC in yaml and enable it in client, all research will be researchable and excluded from the generator.

options.name: OdysseyEnabled
options.description: Enable the Odyssey DLC. If you disable any DLC in yaml and enable it in client, all research will be researchable and excluded from the generator.

options.name: StartingResearchLevel
options.description: If "none" is selected, the player will have access to NO research, regardless of starting scenario. Tribal and Crashlanded will give the player those starting research (regardless of starting scenario.)

options.name: BonusResearchItems
options.description: How many bonus items will you receive when a research (that unlocks items) is received. For example, if this is set to 2, you may receive a jacket and a flophat when you receive Complex Clothing. If set to -1, you'll receive one of everything, otherwise, you'll receive any number at random (note: This means setting it to higher than 1 may result in getting, say, 5 Harps when you receive Harp.)

options.name: ResearchScoutType
options.description: How research project scouting works - this will show you what items research projects will send. "None" will show no information for all research. "Summary" options will show the player name and item type (Chris's progressive item). "Fullitem" options will show player name and item name (Chris's progressive item, Master Sword). "Available" options will show only if the research can be started now. "All" options will hint all research.

options.name: ResearchScoutSecretTraps
options.description: If turned on, the game will lie about scouted trap items. If the scout type shows the player and item name, all traps will be replaced by a random progression item from another player (instead of showing Alex's freeze trap, it will show Chris's Master Sword.) If the scout type only shows item type, it will simply show traps as progression items.

options.name: NeolithicItemWeight
options.description: How likely it will be that low/no-tech items are chosen for crafting locations. Higher weights make this category more likely. 0 will exempt it from the list. Note that these categories are slightly different than the vanilla "Tech Level," to help account for how challenging they are to craft.

options.name: MedievalItemWeight
options.description: How likely it will be that low-tech items are chosen for crafting locations. Higher weights make this category more likely. 0 will exempt it from the list. Note that these categories are slightly different than the vanilla "Tech Level," to help account for how challenging they are to craft.

options.name: IndustrialItemWeight
options.description: How likely it will be that mid-tech items are chosen for crafting locations. Higher weights make this category more likely. 0 will exempt it from the list. Note that these categories are slightly different than the vanilla "Tech Level," to help account for how challenging they are to craft.

options.name: SpacerItemWeight
options.description: How likely it will be that high-tech items are chosen for crafting locations. Higher weights make this category more likely. 0 will exempt it from the list. Note that these categories are slightly different than the vanilla "Tech Level," to help account for how challenging they are to craft.

options.name: HardToMakeItemWeight
options.description: How likely it will be that complicated items are chosen for crafting locations. These items usually require specific/unique investment, like multiple advanced components and the like. Higher weights make this category more likely. 0 will exempt it from the list. Note that these categories are slightly different than the vanilla "Tech Level," to help account for how challenging they are to craft.

options.name: AnomalyItemWeight
options.description: How likely it will be that Anomaly-specific items are chosen for crafting locations. These items will often require bioferrite, and investment into the anomaly expansion to craft. Higher weights make this category more likely. 0 will exempt it from the list. Note that these categories are slightly different than the vanilla "Tech Level," to help account for how challenging they are to craft.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: exclude_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an important item.

options.name: priority_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an unimportant item.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.
